patterns: What are the college working hours
responses: Most colleges run roughly 9 AM to 4/5 PM. If you tell me your college name, I can help you draft a message to confirm timings.
Typical working hours are around 9 AM - 5 PM, but it depends on departments and timetable.
College timings vary by semester. Check your timetable or notice board for the exact schedule.
If you share whether you're in UG or PG, I can give a more relevant timings checklist.
A safe answer: mornings to late afternoon. Want a template to ask the admin office?

patterns: Do you have AI or ML course
responses: Common programs include CSE, ECE, EEE, Mechanical, Civil, IT, and MBA (varies by college).
Many colleges offer UG and PG programs across engineering and management - check the official prospectus for the exact list.
Yes, CSE is common, and many colleges now include AI/ML electives or specializations.
If you tell me the college and your interest area, I can help you shortlist likely departments.
Want a checklist to compare courses (syllabus, labs, faculty, placements)?
Fees depend on course, category, and quota. Check the latest fee circular or official website for exact numbers.
Typical costs include tuition + exam fee + lab fee (and hostel separately if applicable).
Scholarships may exist based on merit, income, or government schemes. Want a list of documents usually required?
If you share your program (like B.Tech CSE) I can tell you what to ask the college for (fee breakdown).
Hostel fees vary by room type and mess plan. Check hostel office or website.
If you tell me the college name/city, I can help you with a 'how to reach' checklist.
Usually you can reach via bus/metro/auto; check Google Maps for the best route from your location.
Look for landmarks near the college and public transport options (bus stop/railway station).
Share your starting point and city, and I can suggest a route plan to search.
Want a short message template to ask the college for the exact address and directions?

patterns: How do I prepare for placements
responses: For placements: focus on DSA basics, projects, resume, and mock interviews. Start small and be consistent.
A good plan: 1) aptitude, 2) DSA, 3) core subjects, 4) projects, 5) resume + LinkedIn, 6) mock interviews.
Companies vary by year. Ask for last year's placement report and top recruiters list.
Build 2 strong projects (one full-stack or ML) and be ready to explain them clearly.
Want a 4-week placement prep schedule?
Machine Learning is a way for computers to learn patterns from data to make predictions or decisions.
AI is the broader goal (machines acting intelligently). ML is one approach to achieve AI using data-driven learning.
Deep Learning is a type of ML using neural networks with many layers, often great for images/text.
NLP (Natural Language Processing) helps computers understand and work with human language.
Want a simple real-life example (spam detection, recommendations, face recognition)?

patterns: How to prepare for ML interview
responses: For ML interviews: know basics (metrics, overfitting, bias-variance), model intuition, and be able to explain a project end-to-end.
Overfitting means the model memorizes training data and performs poorly on new data. Fix with more data, regularization, and validation.
Precision vs Recall: precision is 'how many predicted positives are correct', recall is 'how many actual positives we found'.
Bias-variance tradeoff: high bias underfits, high variance overfits. Aim for balance with proper model complexity.
TF-IDF gives higher weight to words that are frequent in a document but rare across documents - useful for text classification.
